info.title: ClipN API
info.description: AI-powered long-form video to short-form clips

post.description:
Upload a video file for processing.

Optional `client_id` query param scopes the video to a studio client.
When omitted, the video belongs to the operator's own workspace.

get.description:
List videos for the authenticated user.

Scoping rules:
  - scope="all" (default): every video the operator can see
  - scope="operator": only videos without a client (the operator's personal work)
  - client_id=<id>: only videos for that specific client
Get video details (must belong to the authenticated user).

delete.description: Delete a video and all associated data (must belong to user).

get.description:
Server-Sent Events endpoint that streams new clips as they become ready.

Authentication: token passed as ?token= query param (EventSource cannot
send Authorization headers). Token is the same session token from sign-in.

List all clips for a video (must belong to user).

By default variants are hidden — they show up as second-class items
under their parent via GET /clips/{id}/variants. Pass
`include_variants=true` to get the flat list for debugging.

post.description: Trigger clip generation for a video.

get.description: Get the transcript for a video.

post.description:
Request cancellation of an in-progress video job.

Cooperative: flags the job so the pipeline stops at its next checkpoint
(before transcription, before analysis, or before the next clip render).
A transcription already running can't be interrupted mid-call, so during
that phase the stop takes effect once it finishes. Clips already rendered
are kept.

get.description: Get clip details (must belong to user).

delete.description:
Delete a clip (must belong to user).

Cascades to variants: every Clip row with variant_of_clip_id == clip_id is
removed alongside the parent, plus their on-disk files. Without this,
variants would orphan — invisible in the UI (filtered out of all lists)
but still consuming disk and DB rows indefinitely.

Note: a proper DB-level ON DELETE CASCADE is preferred at scale; we're
doing app-level cascade here because SQLite can't alter FK constraints
in place. See docs/pre-launch-checklist.md.

get.description:
Return every stored variant of a parent clip.

Lets the video detail UI re-hydrate the variants panel after a reload
instead of forgetting anything the user generated last session.
Variants of variants aren't a thing (see create_variants guard), so
each returned row is always a direct child of `clip_id`.

schema.title: Response List Variants Api V1 Clips  Clip Id  Variants Get

post.description:
Create N stylistic variants of a clip.

Re-renders the same time range with different combinations of:
caption style, reframe mode, and hook mode. Does NOT re-transcribe.

get.description:
Download the final clip file in the requested aspect ratio.

The original clip is 9:16. Other aspect ratios are generated on-demand
using FFmpeg letterbox with blurred background fill, then cached.
Get clip thumbnail.

put.description:
Re-render a clip with new start/end timestamps.

Reuses the source video, existing caption style/reframe mode.
Does NOT re-run highlight detection or transcription.

post.description: Submit a video URL for download and processing.

get.description: List connected social accounts.

schema.title: Response List Connections Api V1 User Connections Get

post.description: Publish a clip to a connected social platform.

get.description: List all publications for a clip (must belong to user).

schema.title: Response List Publications Api V1 Clips  Clip Id  Publications Get

get.description: Get performance analytics for a clip (must belong to user).
Get overview analytics for the authenticated user's clips.

post.description: Trigger metrics sync from all platforms (for this user).
Submit multiple URLs for batch processing.
Upload multiple video files for batch processing.

get.description: Return available content vertical profiles.
Return one-click generation templates.
Return all available caption styles grouped by category.
Return supported reframe modes.
Return supported aspect ratios for clip output.
Fetch a brand kit — operator default when `client_id` is omitted.
Serve the authenticated user's logo image. Accepts token via ?token= query param since tags cannot send Authorization headers. `client_id` picks the per-client kit; omit for the operator default.

post.description:
Onboard a presenter avatar: upload a still photo, register it with
HeyGen as a talking photo, and store the returned id on the brand kit.

Unlike the rendering endpoints (which degrade gracefully when HeyGen is
down), this one genuinely REQUIRES HeyGen — there's nothing to do without
it — so it returns a clear 503 when no key is configured rather than
silently no-op'ing.

delete.description:
Forget the onboarded avatar. Leaves any local upload on disk but clears
the HeyGen id + disables avatar intros for this kit.

post.description:
Queue a rebrand of every ready clip scoped to the given brand kit.

Scope rules:
  - client_id is None → only operator-owned clips (video.client_id is NULL)
    get rebranded with the operator-default kit
  - client_id is set → only that client's clips are rebranded with that
    client's kit

Never cross-applies — a Peter Drury kit cannot accidentally touch your
own personal clips and vice versa.

get.description:
How many clips within the given brand kit's scope could be rebranded.

Scope matches the `/apply-to-existing` endpoint — operator-default clips
when `client_id` is omitted, or only that client's clips when set.

schema.title: Response List Keys Api V1 Api Keys Get

post.description: Revoke all sessions for the caller's user — simple and safe for MVP.

get.description: Serve the authenticated user's avatar. Accepts ?token= query param since tags can't send Authorization headers.

schema.title: Response List Sessions Api V1 User Sessions Get

post.description: Revoke every session for this user EXCEPT the one making the request.

get.description:
List background operations scoped to the current user.

Filters:
- `video_id`: only operations for this video
- `stage`: exact stage (e.g. "clip.render", "brand.rebrand_clip")
- `status`: queued | running | completed | failed | retrying

SSE stream of operation status changes.

EventSource cannot send Authorization headers, so the session token is
accepted via `?token=` query param — same pattern as the clips stream.

schema.title: Response List Clients Api V1 Clients Get

delete.description:
Soft-archive — flip status to archived instead of dropping rows.

Videos and clips under the client stay intact so the operator keeps the
ability to download delivered work. Hard delete is deferred until we
have a retention/archival policy.

get.description:
Operator-authed view of clips belonging to a client.

Optional `status` filter accepts any single clip_status value (e.g.
`pending_review`, `approved`, `rejected`, `revision_requested`, `ready`).
Omit to get every clip for the client, ordered newest-first.

Variants are hidden by default so an operator's "pending review" badge
reflects parent clips, not 40 rows that came from 10 parents × 4 styles.

schema.title: Response List Client Clips Api V1 Clients  Client Id  Clips Get

post.description:
Generate a magic-link token and email it to the client.

Returns the full token plaintext ONCE. After this response, only the
prefix is retrievable (so the operator can recognize the invite in
their list). Losing the token means creating a fresh invite.

get.description: Recent approve / reject / revision actions for this client's clips.

post.description:
Exchange a magic-link token for a session summary.

Frontends call this on page load to verify the token and fetch the
client display name before rendering the portal shell.

get.description:
Return every clip belonging to the token's client, bucketed by review state.

Splitting into buckets server-side lets the UI render without re-iterating
over a flat list, and makes the shape of the response itself a contract —
any future UI knows exactly which states exist.

post.description:
Approve / reject / request-revision a single clip.

Writes the action to clip_reviews (append-only log for future scoring
models) AND updates the clip.status (so the operator's dashboard sees
the decision immediately).

schema.title: Response List Sources Api V1 Ingestion Sources Get

post.description: Synchronous one-off poll — useful for testing before auto-poller is enabled.
